tagline: Calculate tip and split bills between friends
keywords: tip calculator, bill split, gratuity, restaurant tip
calcFn: var tip = bill * (tipPct/100); var total = bill + tip; var perPerson = total / people; return {tip: dollar(tip), total: dollar(total), perPerson: dollar(perPerson)};

faq.a: 15% for adequate, 18-20% for good, 20%+ for excellent service.
Either is acceptable. Most people tip on the full bill including tax.

formulas.name: Per Person
formulas.explanation: Total including tip divided equally.

tagline: Calculate percentages, changes, and differences
keywords: percentage, percent change, percentage difference, math
calcFn: var difference = newValue - oldValue; var change = oldValue !== 0 ? (difference / oldValue) * 100 : 0; var ratio = oldValue !== 0 ? (newValue / oldValue) * 100 : 0; return {change: (change >= 0 ? '+' : '') + fmt(change,1), difference: fmt(difference,2), ratio: fmt(ratio,1)};

faq.a: (New - Old) / Old × 100 gives percentage increase or decrease.

faq.q: What is the difference between change and difference?
faq.a: Change is relative (%), difference is absolute (same unit as the values).

formulas.name: % Change
formulas.explanation: Percentage increase or decrease from original.

tagline: Calculate snow load on your roof
keywords: snow load, roof load, snow weight, structural load
calcFn: var densities = {'Fresh/Fluffy (3 lb/sqft per ft)': 3, 'Settled (10 lb/sqft per ft)': 10, 'Packed/Wet (15 lb/sqft per ft)': 15, 'Ice Layer (57 lb/sqft per ft)': 57}; var density = densities[snowType] || 10; var psfLoad = (depth / 12) * density; var totalLoad = psfLoad * area; var warning = psfLoad > 30 ? 'DANGER: Exceeds most residential roof limits. Consider snow removal.' : psfLoad > 20 ? 'Heavy load - monitor closely' : 'Within typical limits'; return {psfLoad: fmt(psfLoad,1), totalLoad: fmt(totalLoad,0), warning: warning};

faq.q: How much snow can a roof hold?
faq.a: Most roofs handle 20-40 psf. Check local building codes for your area.

faq.q: When should I remove roof snow?
faq.a: When accumulation exceeds 2 feet of settled snow or visible sagging.

formulas.name: Snow Load
formulas.explanation: Snow depth in feet times density per square foot.

tagline: Calculate grout needed for tile installation
keywords: grout, tile grout, grout coverage, tile installation
calcFn: var tilesPerSqFt = 144 / (tileSize * tileSize); var groutVolume = area * tilesPerSqFt * groutWidth * tileThickness * tileSize * 2 / 144; var groutLbs = groutVolume * 100 * 0.8; var bags = Math.ceil(groutLbs / 25); return {groutLbs: fmt(groutLbs,1), bags: fmt(bags,0)};

faq.q: How much grout do I need?
faq.a: Depends on tile size, grout line width, and tile thickness. Larger tiles use less grout.
Sanded for lines 1/8 inch or wider, unsanded for narrower lines.

formulas.name: Grout Volume
formulas.formula: Based on total grout line length × width × depth
formulas.explanation: Total linear feet of grout lines times cross-section area.

tagline: Calculate the electricity cost of running any appliance
keywords: electricity cost, power consumption, kWh, appliance cost
calcFn: var kwhPerDay = (watts * hoursPerDay) / 1000; var dailyCost = kwhPerDay * rate; var monthlyCost = dailyCost * 30; var annualCost = dailyCost * 365; return {dailyCost: dollar(dailyCost), monthlyCost: dollar(monthlyCost), annualCost: dollar(annualCost)};

faq.q: How do I find appliance wattage?
faq.a: Check the label on the appliance or look up the model specifications.
HVAC, water heater, and dryer are typically the biggest electricity users.

formulas.name: Cost
formulas.explanation: Convert to kWh then multiply by your rate.

tagline: Calculate the number of days between two dates
keywords: date difference, days between, date calculator, countdown
calcFn: var d1 = new Date(year1, month1-1, day1); var d2 = new Date(year2, month2-1, day2); var diff = Math.abs(d2 - d1); var days = Math.floor(diff / (1000*60*60*24)); var weeks = Math.floor(days / 7); var months = days / 30.44; return {days: fmt(days,0), weeks: fmt(weeks,0), months: fmt(months,1)};

faq.q: How do I calculate days between dates?
faq.a: Subtract the earlier date from the later date to get the difference in days.

faq.q: Does this account for leap years?
faq.a: Yes, the calculation automatically handles leap years.

formulas.name: Date Difference
formulas.explanation: Absolute difference between two dates.

tagline: Convert time between major world time zones
keywords: time zone, time converter, world clock, timezone
calcFn: var diff = toZone - fromZone; var newHour = ((hour + diff) % 24 + 24) % 24; var dayChange = hour + diff >= 24 ? ' (+1 day)' : hour + diff < 0 ? ' (-1 day)' : ''; var ampm = newHour >= 12 ? 'PM' : 'AM'; var displayHour = newHour > 12 ? newHour - 12 : newHour === 0 ? 12 : newHour; var convertedTime = displayHour + ':' + (minute < 10 ? '0' : '') + minute + ' ' + ampm + dayChange; return {convertedTime: convertedTime, difference: (diff >= 0 ? '+' : '') + fmt(diff,0)};

faq.a: Coordinated Universal Time, the global time standard. EST is UTC-5, JST is UTC+9.

faq.q: Do all zones shift for daylight saving?
faq.a: No, many countries and US states do not observe daylight saving time.

formulas.name: Conversion
formulas.formula: New Time = Original + (To Zone - From Zone)
formulas.explanation: Add the difference in UTC offsets.

tagline: Convert between hex color codes and RGB values
keywords: hex to RGB, color converter, web colors, CSS colors
calcFn: var hex = '#' + ('0'+red.toString(16)).slice(-2) + ('0'+green.toString(16)).slice(-2) + ('0'+blue.toString(16)).slice(-2); var r = red/255; var g = green/255; var b = blue/255; var max = Math.max(r,g,b); var min = Math.min(r,g,b); var l = (max+min)/2; var s = max===min ? 0 : l > 0.5 ? (max-min)/(2-max-min) : (max-min)/(max+min); var h = 0; if(max!==min){if(max===r)h=(g-b)/(max-min)+(g<b?6:0);else if(max===g)h=(b-r)/(max-min)+2;else h=(r-g)/(max-min)+4;h/=6;} var hsl = fmt(h*360,0) + ', ' + fmt(s*100,0) + '%, ' + fmt(l*100,0) + '%'; return {hex: hex.toUpperCase(), rgb: red+', '+green+', '+blue, hsl: hsl};

faq.a: A 6-digit hexadecimal representation of RGB color values used in web design.
Hue (0-360°), Saturation (0-100%), Lightness (0-100%) - often more intuitive than RGB.

formulas.name: Hex Conversion
formulas.explanation: Each color channel represented as two hexadecimal digits.

tagline: Estimate JSON payload size for API requests
keywords: JSON size, payload, API, bandwidth, data size
calcFn: var fieldOverhead = 15; var bytesPerRecord = fieldsPerRecord * (avgFieldLength + fieldOverhead); var totalBytes = records * bytesPerRecord + 100; var sizeKB = totalBytes / 1024; var sizeMB = sizeKB / 1024; var transferTime = (totalBytes * 8) / 10000000; return {sizeKB: fmt(sizeKB,1), sizeMB: fmt(sizeMB,2), transferTime: fmt(transferTime,2)};

faq.q: How big is a typical API response?
faq.a: Varies widely: 1-10 KB for simple, 100+ KB for data-heavy responses.
Yes, gzip typically reduces JSON size by 70-80%.

formulas.name: Payload Size
formulas.explanation: Total bytes from record count, field count, and average lengths.

tagline: Calculate battery runtime from capacity and load
keywords: battery life, runtime, mAh, battery capacity, discharge
calcFn: var effectiveCapacity = capacity * (efficiency/100); var hours = effectiveCapacity / loadMa; var days = hours / 24; return {hours: fmt(hours,1), days: fmt(days,1)};

faq.a: Capacity (mAh) divided by current draw (mA) equals hours of runtime.
Voltage regulators and circuits waste 10-25% of battery energy as heat.

formulas.name: Runtime
formulas.explanation: Effective capacity divided by current consumption.

tagline: Look up maximum amperage for wire gauge sizes
keywords: wire ampacity, wire gauge, AWG, current capacity, electrical wire
calcFn: var amps = {'14 AWG': 15, '12 AWG': 20, '10 AWG': 30, '8 AWG': 40, '6 AWG': 55, '4 AWG': 70, '2 AWG': 95, '1/0 AWG': 125}; var ampacity = amps[gauge] || 20; var tempDerate = {'Normal (<86°F)': 1, 'Warm (86-104°F)': 0.88, 'Hot (>104°F)': 0.75}; ampacity = Math.floor(ampacity * (tempDerate[ambientTemp] || 1)); var breakerSize = amps[gauge] || 20; var uses = {'14 AWG': 'Lighting, general outlets', '12 AWG': 'Kitchen, bathroom outlets', '10 AWG': 'Dryer, water heater', '8 AWG': 'Range, large AC', '6 AWG': 'Subpanel feeder', '4 AWG': 'Large subpanel', '2 AWG': 'Main feeder', '1/0 AWG': '100A service'}; var typicalUse = uses[gauge] || ''; return {ampacity: fmt(ampacity,0), breakerSize: fmt(breakerSize,0), typicalUse: typicalUse};

faq.a: 12 AWG minimum for 20-amp circuits per NEC code.
Yes, high ambient temperatures reduce ampacity by 12-25%.

formulas.name: Derated Ampacity
formulas.explanation: Standard ampacity reduced for high ambient temperatures.

tagline: Estimate the cost of moving to a new home
keywords: moving cost, relocation, moving estimate, movers cost
calcFn: var baseCosts = {'DIY (truck rental)': [200,500], 'Hiring Movers': [800,2000], 'Full Service (packing included)': [2000,5000]}; var range = baseCosts[moveType] || [800,2000]; var sizeMult = 0.5 + bedrooms * 0.5; var distanceMult = distance > 100 ? 1 + (distance/1000) : 1; var lowEstimate = range[0] * sizeMult * distanceMult; var highEstimate = range[1] * sizeMult * distanceMult; var timeline = distance > 500 ? '2-5 days' : distance > 50 ? '1-2 days' : 'Same day'; return {lowEstimate: dollar(lowEstimate), highEstimate: dollar(highEstimate), timeline: timeline};

faq.a: Local moves: $800-2,500. Long-distance: $2,000-8,000+. DIY is cheapest.

faq.q: When is the cheapest time to move?
faq.a: Mid-month, mid-week, and September-April are typically cheapest.

formulas.name: Estimate
formulas.formula: Cost = Base × Size Factor × Distance Factor
formulas.explanation: Base rate adjusted for home size and distance.

tagline: Compare the financial impact of renting versus buying a home
keywords: rent vs buy, home buying, renting, mortgage, housing cost
calcFn: var downPayment = homePrice * (downPct/100); var loanAmount = homePrice - downPayment; var r = mortgageRate/100/12; var n = 30 * 12; var mortgage = loanAmount * r / (1 - Math.pow(1+r,-n)); var monthlyOwn = mortgage + (homePrice * 0.0125/12) + (homePrice * 0.005/12); var totalRentCost = monthlyRent * years * 12 * 1.03; var totalBuyCost = monthlyOwn * years * 12 + downPayment; var appreciation = homePrice * Math.pow(1.03, years) - homePrice; var equity = downPayment + appreciation; var netBuyCost = totalBuyCost - equity; var verdict = netBuyCost < totalRentCost ? 'Buying is cheaper by ' + dollar(totalRentCost - netBuyCost) : 'Renting is cheaper by ' + dollar(netBuyCost - totalRentCost); return {monthlyMortgage: dollar(mortgage), totalRentCost: dollar(totalRentCost), totalBuyCost: dollar(netBuyCost), verdict: verdict};

faq.q: Is it better to rent or buy?
faq.a: Depends on how long you'll stay, local prices, interest rates, and rent costs.

faq.q: How long should I plan to own?
faq.a: Generally need 5-7 years to break even on buying costs (closing costs, selling fees).

formulas.name: Monthly Mortgage
formulas.explanation: Standard amortization formula for fixed-rate mortgage.

tagline: Calculate file download time based on internet speed
keywords: download time, internet speed, file size, bandwidth
calcFn: var bits = fileSize * 8 * 1024; var seconds = bits / speed; var minutes = seconds / 60; var realistic = minutes / 0.7; return {seconds: fmt(seconds,0), minutes: fmt(minutes,1), realistic: fmt(realistic,1)};

faq.q: Why is my download slower than my speed?
faq.a: Real-world throughput is typically 60-80% of advertised speed due to overhead.
Mbps is megabits (speed). MBps is megabytes (file size). 1 MBps = 8 Mbps.

formulas.name: Download Time
formulas.explanation: File size in megabits divided by speed in megabits per second.

tagline: Calculate how strong your password is and how long to crack it
keywords: password strength, password security, crack time, entropy
calcFn: var pool = 0; pool += hasUpper === 'Yes' ? 26 : 0; pool += hasLower === 'Yes' ? 26 : 0; pool += hasNumbers === 'Yes' ? 10 : 0; pool += hasSymbols === 'Yes' ? 32 : 0; pool = Math.max(pool, 1); var entropy = Math.log2(Math.pow(pool, length)); var combinations = Math.pow(pool, length); var perSecond = 10000000000; var seconds = combinations / perSecond; var crackTime = seconds < 1 ? 'Instant' : seconds < 60 ? fmt(seconds,0) + ' seconds' : seconds < 3600 ? fmt(seconds/60,0) + ' minutes' : seconds < 86400 ? fmt(seconds/3600,0) + ' hours' : seconds < 31536000 ? fmt(seconds/86400,0) + ' days' : seconds < 3.15e10 ? fmt(seconds/31536000,0) + ' years' : fmt(seconds/31536000,0) + ' years'; var strength = entropy >= 80 ? 'Very Strong' : entropy >= 60 ? 'Strong' : entropy >= 40 ? 'Moderate' : entropy >= 28 ? 'Weak' : 'Very Weak'; return {entropy: fmt(entropy,0), crackTime: crackTime, strength: strength};

faq.a: 12+ characters with mixed case, numbers, and symbols. Length matters most.

faq.q: How fast can passwords be cracked?
faq.a: Modern GPUs can try 10 billion combinations per second against common hashes.

formulas.name: Entropy
formulas.explanation: Bits of entropy based on character pool and password length.

tagline: Calculate blocks, mortar, and rebar for a block wall
keywords: concrete block, CMU, block wall, masonry, block calculator
calcFn: var sqft = length * height; var blocksPerSqFt = 1.125; var blocks = Math.ceil(sqft * blocksPerSqFt * 1.05); var mortarBags = Math.ceil(blocks / 30); var verticals = Math.ceil(length / 4) + 1; var rebarLength = verticals * (height + 2); return {blocks: fmt(blocks,0), mortarBags: fmt(mortarBags,0), rebarLength: fmt(rebarLength,0)};

faq.q: How many blocks per square foot?
faq.a: Standard 8×16 blocks: 1.125 blocks per square foot of wall.
About 30-35 standard blocks per 60-lb bag of mortar mix.

formulas.name: Blocks
formulas.explanation: Blocks per square foot with 5% waste factor.

tagline: Estimate your take-home pay after taxes and deductions
keywords: paycheck, take home pay, salary, net pay, after tax
calcFn: var periods = {'Weekly': 52, 'Bi-Weekly': 26, 'Semi-Monthly': 24, 'Monthly': 12}; var annualGross = grossPay * (periods[payFrequency] || 26); var effectiveRate = filingStatus === 'Single' ? (annualGross > 95375 ? 0.22 : annualGross > 44725 ? 0.17 : annualGross > 11600 ? 0.12 : 0.10) : (annualGross > 190750 ? 0.22 : annualGross > 89450 ? 0.17 : annualGross > 23200 ? 0.12 : 0.10); var federalTax = grossPay * effectiveRate; var fica = grossPay * 0.0765; var stateTax = grossPay * (state/100); var netPay = grossPay - federalTax - fica - stateTax; return {federalTax: dollar(federalTax), fica: dollar(fica), stateTax: dollar(stateTax), netPay: dollar(netPay)};

faq.q: How much of my paycheck goes to taxes?
faq.a: Typically 25-35% for federal income tax, FICA, and state taxes combined.
7.65% for Social Security (6.2%) and Medicare (1.45%).

formulas.name: Net Pay
formulas.formula: Net = Gross - Federal Tax - FICA - State Tax
formulas.explanation: Gross pay minus all tax withholdings.

tagline: Calculate real investment returns after inflation
keywords: inflation, real return, purchasing power, inflation adjusted
calcFn: var realReturn = ((1 + nominalReturn/100) / (1 + inflation/100) - 1) * 100; var nominalValue = investment * Math.pow(1 + nominalReturn/100, years); var realValue = investment * Math.pow(1 + realReturn/100, years); var purchasingPowerLoss = nominalValue - realValue; return {realReturn: fmt(realReturn,2), nominalValue: dollar(nominalValue), realValue: dollar(realValue), purchasingPowerLoss: dollar(purchasingPowerLoss)};

faq.a: Your investment return after subtracting inflation. It shows actual purchasing power gained.

faq.q: Why does inflation matter for investing?
faq.a: At 3% inflation, $100 today is worth only $74 in 10 years.

formulas.name: Fisher Equation
formulas.explanation: Exact formula for inflation-adjusted returns.

tagline: Calculate your Body Mass Index
keywords: BMI, body mass index, weight, health, obesity
calcFn: var totalInches = heightFt * 12 + heightIn; var bmi = (weight / (totalInches * totalInches)) * 703; var category = bmi < 18.5 ? 'Underweight' : bmi < 25 ? 'Normal' : bmi < 30 ? 'Overweight' : 'Obese'; var lowHealthy = Math.round(18.5 * totalInches * totalInches / 703); var highHealthy = Math.round(24.9 * totalInches * totalInches / 703); var healthyRange = lowHealthy + ' - ' + highHealthy; return {bmi: fmt(bmi,1), category: category, healthyRange: healthyRange};

faq.a: 18.5-24.9 is normal. BMI doesn't account for muscle mass or body composition.
It's a screening tool, not a diagnostic one. Athletes may have high BMI with low body fat.

formulas.name: BMI
formulas.formula: BMI = (Weight in lbs / Height in inches²) × 703
formulas.explanation: Weight relative to height squared.

tagline: Calculate your calorie deficit for weight loss
keywords: calorie deficit, weight loss, TDEE, calories, diet
calcFn: var weightKg = weight * 0.453592; var heightCm = height * 2.54; var bmr = sex === 'Male' ? 10*weightKg + 6.25*heightCm - 5*age + 5 : 10*weightKg + 6.25*heightCm - 5*age - 161; var actMult = {'Sedentary': 1.2, 'Light Exercise': 1.375, 'Moderate Exercise': 1.55, 'Heavy Exercise': 1.725}; var tdee = bmr * (actMult[activity] || 1.375); var deficitCals = goalLbs * 500; var deficit = Math.max(tdee - deficitCals, 1200); var weeksToGoal = 20 / goalLbs; return {tdee: fmt(tdee,0), deficit: fmt(deficit,0), weeksToGoal: fmt(weeksToGoal,0)};

faq.q: How big should my calorie deficit be?
faq.a: 500 cal/day deficit = ~1 lb/week loss. Don't go below 1200 cal/day.
Total Daily Energy Expenditure - the calories you burn including activity.

formulas.name: TDEE
formulas.explanation: Basal metabolic rate scaled by activity level.
